Art title 1: Knock and The Door Will Be Opened (by Anthony Gurrola)
Artist Statement 1:
Your brokenness isn't your worth.

Your design is.

Brokenness can come in many ways,

By way of sickness,

Tragedy,

Abuse,

Addiction,
Loss,

Depression,

The list can go on, but nothing on that list can give or take away your value.

No matter how much this fallen world breaks you,

It cannot take away from your design.

You were created by the creator.

He looked at you before the creation of the Earth, and He said

"It is good."

Nothing can steal that.

No heights,

No depths,

No pain,

No suffering,

No brokenness can separate you from the love of God.

That's where your worth is found.

In His arms.

You are found in the infinite value of His heart and mind.

You are His every heartbeat,

You are His greatest thought,

You are His greatest creation.

You are infinity, inescapably valuable to Him.

The Maker of Heaven and Earth,

The one who paints the sky and moves the mountains says you are His favorite.

Even if we as clay, are broken by the very hand of the Potter,

Know that you're in good hands.

We just went off our design a little, and He is determined to finish the good work.

You are a treasure in the eyes of the Maker.

No matter how many cuts, cracks, or brushes,

You are His.

(Anthony Gurrola)
excerpt:
Why pray for something to happen if God already has a better plan? After all, he knows better than I. Yet God still says, pray for what you want to happen and pray with confidence, lest we become as weak as a wave blown by the wind.

Once there was a father with three children. The oldest, Dan saw his two younger brothers fighting all too often. At first he waited for his dad to do something about it. His father DID know a good way to solve the problem but he was such a good father that he also saw an opportunity for Dan to grow and learn something through this situation. So he waited.

Finally, Dan pleaded to his father, "Dad, please do something about all this arguing!"

Because his father knew that Dan would be a father himself someday he asked, "What would you have me do, Dan?" And Dan thought and thought and asked his dad to make each of this brothers do something nice for each other and see how it made them feel.

This may not have been the same decision the father would have made but the father knew that it was a good decision and would have a good outcome too. And Dan would learn better and better in time. But Dan's father was very proud when he saw that his son was growing up to be a wise, noble man, after his own image.

Maybe this is why God asks us to pray for things- because he is teaching us to think like Him. "What would you do if you were a wise, caring, spirit like me. Good, now let's get to making you into one."

Raw Spoon
